claim: Traditional centralized data stacks (ETL to single warehouse) don't scale for AI agents; agents need distributed, sandboxed access to real-time data across OLTP, document stores, and message buses.
actionable: For agent-heavy architectures, move from centralized ETL pipelines to federated data access; give agents localized, replicated datasets rather than direct database access.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack

claim: Each agent should have its own sandboxed data stack with federated access to backend systems (databases, APIs, document stores) rather than direct access, improving security and isolation.
actionable: Implement agent data isolation: replicate working datasets into local DuckDB/SQLite instances that agents query directly, preventing direct connections to production databases.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack

claim: Spice AI provides federated SQL query across heterogeneous backend data stores (Parquet, Snowflake, MySQL, MongoDB, Elasticsearch) with local acceleration via DuckDB/SQLite/Vortex.
actionable: Evaluate Spice AI for agents needing consistent, federated SQL query interface across multiple data stores with local acceleration.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack

claim: Agents querying production systems directly cause orders-of-magnitude load increases; GitHub experienced significant outages due to agent load on their infrastructure.
actionable: Never grant agents direct access to production databases; always add rate limiting, caching, or replicated datasets as intermediaries.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack

claim: OpenClaw with a Spice data stack can perform real-time SRE incident investigation and mitigation by querying logs, metrics, databases, and troubleshooting guides (unstructured data) simultaneously.
actionable: For operational agents, provide access to both structured data (metrics, logs) and unstructured data (runbooks, guides) through a unified federated query interface.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack

claim: AI agents destroyed production data in real incidents due to insufficient access controls; agents need role-based access and query sandboxing to prevent accidental or malicious data destruction.
actionable: Implement strict query sandboxing and row/column-level access control for agents; use read-only replicas for most agent workloads, only grant write access where explicitly required.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Luke Kim: The Agent Data Stack
